word_list.word: if you trust before you try, you may repent before you die
word_list.pronunciation: 瑟f ju藧 t晒藞蕦st b瑟f藢o藧晒 ju藧 t晒藞a瑟 ju藧 m藞e瑟 晒瑟p藞蓻nt b瑟f藢o藧晒 ju藧 d藞a瑟

senses.definition: used to advise against trusting or believing in someone or something without first verifying their reliability or effectiveness, as it may lead to regret or negative consequences in the future

word_list.word: you should know a man seven years before you stir his fire

senses.definition: used to advise patience and careful consideration in forming close relationships, indicating that trust and loyalty are built over time and through experience

senses.definition: used to imply that a person who shares gossip with one cannot be trusted, as they are likely to spread gossip about one as well

word_list.word: promises are like pie [crust], (they are|) made to be broken

senses.definition: used to suggest that promises are often insincere or easily broken, and should not be relied upon too heavily

word_list.word: be slow to promise, but quick to (perform|deliver)
word_list.pronunciation: bi藧 sl藞o蕣 t蓹 p晒藞蓱藧m瑟s b藢蕦t kw藞瑟k t蓹 p蓺f藞蓴藧晒m 蓴藧晒 d瑟l藞瑟v蓺

senses.definition: used to emphasize that one should not make promises lightly or without careful consideration, but once a promise is made, it should be fulfilled promptly and without delay

word_list.word: jam tomorrow and jam yesterday, but never jam today
word_list.pronunciation: d蕭藞忙m t蓹m藞蓴藧晒o蕣 忙nd d蕭藞忙m j藞蓻st蓺d藢e瑟 b藢蕦t n藞蓻v蓺 d蕭藞忙m t蓹d藞e瑟

senses.definition: used to caution people against being deceived or distracted by empty promises that offer future rewards or benefits that are not fulfilled in the present moment

word_list.word: a fool's speech is a bubble of air
word_list.pronunciation: 蓯 f藞u藧lz sp藞i藧t蕛 瑟z 蓯 b藞蕦b蓹l 蕦v 藞蓻晒

senses.definition: used to suggest that the words spoken by a fool lack substance or depth, and therefore should not be trusted or taken seriously

examples.x: Don't pay attention to what he's saying, a fool's speech is a bubble of air.

word_list.word: bees that have honey in their mouths have stings in their tails
word_list.pronunciation: b藞i藧z 冒忙t h忙v h藞蕦ni 瑟n 冒蓻晒 m藞a蕣冒z h忙v st藞瑟艐z 瑟n 冒蓻晒 t藞e瑟lz

senses.definition: used to suggest that people or things that seem harmless or kind may conceal hidden dangers or harmful intentions, advising one to be wary and cautious of potential risks or threats

word_list.pronunciation: 伞藞蕣d 蓯k藞a蕣nt瑟艐 m藢e瑟ks f蓴藧晒 蓴藧晒 伞藞蕣d f晒藞蓻ndz

senses.definition: used to imply when people are honest and accurate in their accounting, they are less likely to have disagreements about money which can help to keep friendships peaceful and harmonious

examples.x: I learned the hard way that good accounting makes good friends after a disagreement with my roommate over unpaid bills almost ruined our friendship, but we were able to resolve the issue by keeping better track of our expenses.
